frame_label: Misogyny Jokes: Childbearing/Rearing as Women's Responsibility
generated_comment:
Mohammed: Arre bhai, job-vob theek hai, but once children come, mother's time is mother's time only. Father can help, of course, but bachchon ko sambhalna, school, doctor, all that naturally falls on the woman.
Wife: Haan, career can continue later also.
Mohammed: Bas wahi, bachpan nikal gaya toh wapas nahi aata.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covertly misogynistic because it naturalizes childcare and domestic management as primarily the mother's duty while framing fathers as merely 'helping.' It encodes the surface manifestations of career sacrifice framing and the assumption that school/doctor/childcare responsibilities belong to women.
persona_text: You are Mohammed. Age 52. Married. A Muslim man in Hyderabad. Your 26 year old daughter Sana has just been offered a two year fellowship abroad for her research. She is excited. You are proud of her. But you are also thinking about her age and the fellowship timeline Ñ she will be 28 when she returns and in your community 28 is already considered late for a girl. You are sitting with your wife after Sana has gone to sleep. You are telling your wife what you think should happen Ñ whether Sana should take the fellowship or whether this is the moment to start looking seriously for a match before she goes any further in the direction she is going.
